title: Archidendron muricarpum
taxonomy: Archidendron muricarpum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and is commonly found in countries such as India, China, and Southeast Asia. The plant is also known as the "thorny nut tree" due to its distinctive seed pods. Archidendron muricarpum is a member of the genus Archidendron, which consists of several species of tropical and subtropical trees and shrubs. The plant's classification is further supported by its morphological and anatomical characteristics, which are consistent with other species in the Fabaceae family.

intro: Archidendron muricarpum, also known as the "Murraya" or "Fish Tail Tree", is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Archidendron muricarpum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
